deck.notes: Primary artifact for this stress eval is a bounded findings summary rather than a deck.

session.notes: Browser session export capturing the weighted WVS stress analysis state.

scores.notes: WVS passed the metadata gate and then completed the worker-backed chunked v3 load in 21 chunks without parse or stability failure.
The successful stress evidence is browser-real, but the broader agent-native and CLI story for this exact file still lacks equivalent execution confidence.
Label-level orientation to happiness, trust, and government was quick, but the run did not fully stress open-ended WVS discovery across hundreds of code-heavy variables.
The browser path handled upload, metadata review, full load, weighted analysis, and session export cleanly, though the repeatable harness still uses in-page store actions.
The run produced a reviewable findings summary, CSV evidence, raw run metadata, and a portable session, but not a more polished first-class stress-report export.
The metadata-first large-file guardrail and automatic chunked routing behaved as intended on a hostile dataset, though the extra handoff still adds workflow friction.
Tight scope control kept the stress run bounded and attributable instead of letting WVS sprawl into ad hoc exploration.

notableFindings: WVS Wave 7 completed the browser metadata gate, then the full chunked v3 load, without requiring fallback to trust.sav.
The worker loaded 97,220 rows in 21 chunks and surfaced 693 variables plus 502 variable sets on the app side.
Bounded weighted analysis showed higher happiness among respondents who said most people can be trusted (90.9% happy or quite happy) than among those who said one must be very careful (84.0%).
blockers: This run validates the browser stress path more strongly than the broader agent-native or CLI story for the same WVS file.
The raw parser metadata count and the surfaced app variable count still need clearer explanation for large-dataset discovery.
followUps: Freeze this run as the Phase 4 stress baseline and move to S4-EVAL-4 synthesis.
Bring MCP and CLI WVS ingestion into line with the now-proven browser path.
Document the heuristic variable-surface expansion layer so large-file discovery remains interpretable.
